test.name: Informant primary/secondary question should route to record-extraction
test.description: The user asks whether the informant on a specific persisted death certificate (src_004) is primary or secondary for the father's name. That is GPS Step 3 evidence classification — owned by record-extraction since the assertion-classification merge (2026-07-11) — not writing a proof conclusion. The message is anchored to project state so a generic informant lecture cannot satisfy it.

input.user_message: Is the informant on src_004 — Patrick's 1908 death certificate — primary or secondary for the father's name? Check what we have on file.

negative.explanation: Judging whether an informant provides primary or secondary information is three-layer evidence classification — record-extraction's job (it writes classifications at extraction and refines them on request; the former assertion-classification skill was merged into it). proof-conclusion uses classifications that already exist on assertions; it does not assign them.

judge_context: Should route to record-extraction (the classification owner since the assertion-classification merge) rather than producing a GPS proof narrative

The question is anchored to persisted project state: src_004 (Patrick's 1908 death certificate) and its father's-name assertion a_013 already exist in research.json with information_quality 'secondary' and informant James Brown (son-in-law). A generic inline lecture about informant classification that never engages what is on file does NOT satisfy the request
Reporting what a_013 already says on file (with attribution to the stored assertion) alongside the record-extraction redirect is acceptable; any write to research.json from this boundary fails
